sources.issuing_organisation: National Institute for Health and Care Excellence
sources.version: NICE guideline NG14; last updated 27 July 2022
sources.jurisdiction: United Kingdom; international evidence requiring UAE skin-cancer pathway adaptation
sources.population: People with a pigmented lesion assessed in secondary or tertiary care and people receiving follow-up after melanoma treatment.
sources.clinical_setting: Specialist dermatology and skin-cancer assessment and surveillance.
sources.applicability_note: Exact for clinician-performed dermoscopy, baseline photography, interval image comparison, full skin and regional-node examination, access to medical photography, and follow-up support. It does not diagnose melanoma or generate biopsy, imaging, treatment, referral, or follow-up intervals.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.3.1–1.3.3 — dermoscopy and baseline photography
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports recording trained-clinician dermoscopy, baseline clinical or dermoscopic photography, and interval comparison for a clinically atypical melanocytic lesion in specialist care.

exact_sections.heading: Recommendations 1.9.1–1.9.11 — follow-up support and examination
exact_sections.evidence_summary: Supports documenting concerns, personalized follow-up context, clinician-performed full skin and regional lymph-node examination, dermoscopy, medical photography, and completed specialist follow-up without generating an interval or scan.
